LAW AND MOTION 302, THE REPORT AND RECOMMENDATION OF THE JUDGE PRO TEM IS ADOPTED IN ITS ENTIRETY. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO QUASH THE SUBPOENAS SERVED ON HIS LAW FIRMS (NASSIRI & JUNG LLP, FELDMAN GALE, AND COZEN O'CONNOR) IS GRANTED, WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO THE ISSUANCE OF NEW SUBPOENAS THAT DO NOT SEEK PRODUCTION OF INFORMATION PROTECTED FROM DISCLOSURE BY THE ATTORNEY-CLIENT PRIVILEGE AND/OR THE ATTORNEY WORK PRODUCT DOCTRINE. (SEE GENERALLY LOS ANGELES COUNTY BOARD OF SUPERVISORS V. SUPERIOR COURT (2016) 2 CAL.5TH 282, 293-300; COUNTY OF LOS ANGELES BD. OF SUPERVISORS V. SUPERIOR COURT (2017) 12 CAL.APP.5TH 1264, 1273-1276 [ON REMAND].) BY FILING THIS ACTION TO ENFORCE HIS WRITTEN INDEMNITY AGREEMENT WITH DEFENDANT, PLAINTIFF DID NOT PUT AT ISSUE PRIVILEGED COMMUNICATIONS WITH HIS ATTORNEYS OR EFFECT AN IMPLIED WAIVER OF THE ATTORNEY-CLIENT PRIVILEGE. BECAUSE THE SUBPOENAS WERE OPPRESSIVE AND DEFENDANT OPPOSED THE MOTION WITHOUT SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION, PLAINTIFF IS AWARDED REASONABLE ATTORNEYS' FEES IN THE AMOUNT OF $9,870. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; COURT REPORTER: MARIA A. TORREANO, CSR#8600, E-MAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM (302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Subpoenas To Plaintiffs Law Firms
Off Calendar Aug-28-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Atty. ROEY Z. RAHMIL, ESQ. of Shartsis Friese LLP (Tel: 415-421-6500) appearing for plaintiff James Gutierrez;
Attys. MARGARET ZIEMIANEK, ESQ. and LYN R. AGRE, ESQ. of Kasowitz Benson Torres LLP (Tel: 415-655-4335) appearing for defendant Oportun Financial Corporation.

Hearing Re: Plaintiff James Gutierrez's Motion To Quash Subpoenas To Plaintiffs Law Firms.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: The report and recommendation of the judge pro tem is adopted in its entirety. Plaintiff's motion to quash the subpoenas served on his law firms (Nassiri & Jung LLP, Feldman Gale, and Cozen O'Connor) is granted, without prejudice to the issuance of new subpoenas that do not seek production of information protected from disclosure by the attorney-client privilege and/or the attorney work product doctrine. (See generally Los Angeles County Board of Supervisors v. Superior Court (2016) 2 Cal.5th 282, 293-300; County of Los Angeles Bd. of Supervisors v. Superior Court (2017) 12 Cal.App.5th 1264, 1273-1276 [on remand].) By filing this action to enforce his written indemnity agreement with defendant, plaintiff did not put at issue privileged communications with his attorneys or effect an implied waiver of the attorney-client privilege. Because the subpoenas were oppressive and defendant opposed the motion without substantial justification, plaintiff is awarded reasonable attorneys' fees in the amount of $9,870.

Order signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano, CSR#8600, e-mail: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)
